direction: Increase indicates elevated IDO1 activity driven by IFN-γ; reflects systemic low-grade inflammation
caution: Interpret as Kyn/Trp ratio rather than absolute value; renal function affects clearance

direction: Increase relative to quinolinic acid indicates a shift toward the neuroprotective IDO1 branch; generally favourable
caution: Ratio to quinolinic acid is more informative than absolute level

direction: Increase indicates shunting toward the neurotoxic IDO1 branch; associated with insulin resistance and neuroinflammation
caution: None specific to this platform

pathway: Gut microbiome / choline and carnitine catabolism
direction: Decrease expected with exercise; reflects gut microbiome remodelling and reduced pro-inflammatory signalling
caution: Strongly diet-dependent; dietary recall data should be collected at both timepoints

direction: Free carnitine may decrease as acylcarnitine production increases with improved fatty acid oxidation during exercise training
caution: Interpret C0 alongside C2 and long-chain acylcarnitines as a ratio

direction: Increase with exercise training; reflects increased mitochondrial flux and improved metabolic flexibility
caution: C2/C0 ratio is the key metric; absolute C2 less informative alone

direction: Decrease with exercise training; elevated C16 at baseline indicates incomplete fatty acid oxidation and insulin resistance
caution: FIA-MS/MS: isomer co-elution possible; treat as class signal

direction: Acute increase post-exercise; chronic training may reduce resting cortisol, reflecting improved stress adaptation
caution: Diurnal variation is large; samples must be collected at a consistent time of day at both T0 and T1

direction: Increase with regular exercise; higher DHEAS/cortisol ratio indicates reduced allostatic load
caution: Age-dependent decline; adjust for age in analysis

direction: Decrease expected with reduced inflammation; elevated ADMA inhibits nitric oxide production and predicts cardiovascular risk
caution: Renal clearance affects levels; eGFR should be recorded

direction: Decrease with B-vitamin sufficiency and exercise; elevated levels indicate methylation impairment and cardiovascular risk
caution: Sample handling critical — must be centrifuged promptly; erythrocyte lysis causes artefactual elevation

direction: Changes reflect mitochondrial TCA flux; increase with improved aerobic capacity and mitochondrial biogenesis
caution: Dietary citrate confounds measurement; 12-hour fast required

direction: Elevated succinate is a danger-associated molecular pattern (DAMP); decrease expected with reduced inflammatory tone
caution: Can accumulate post-exercise acutely; endpoint measure more informative than early timepoints

pathway: Gut microbiome / tryptophan catabolism by intestinal bacteria
direction: Decrease expected with exercise-driven gut microbiome shifts; uremic toxin that activates AhR pro-inflammatory signalling
caution: Renal clearance dependent; confounded in participants with reduced kidney function

direction: Increase expected with exercise; anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective, gut barrier-protective
caution: Inverse of indoxyl sulfate; these two should be reported together as a gut microbiome health index

direction: Exercise increases bile acid diversity and pool size; primary:secondary bile acid ratio reflects gut microbiome health
caution: Dietary fat intake strongly influences bile acid levels; collect dietary data at both timepoints

direction: Increase in DCA/CA ratio indicates improved gut microbiome diversity with exercise; DCA has insulin-sensitising properties
caution: None specific to this platform

pathway: Biogenic amine / gut-brain axis / platelet activation
direction: Increase with exercise; 95% of circulating serotonin is gut-derived; links gut microbiome to mood and metabolic outcomes
caution: Platelet serotonin dominates plasma measurement; collection protocol (EDTA vs serum) affects values substantially

direction: Increase with caloric restriction and exercise; induces autophagy via eIF5A hypusination; anti-ageing associations
caution: Dietary spermidine (wheat germ, fermented foods) confounds endogenous production measurement

direction: Resting lactate decreases with aerobic training; lactate at the same submaximal workload falls as fitness improves
caution: Highly sensitive to sample collection conditions and time-since-last-exercise; standardise pre-sample activity window
